title: Aberdeen Group Litigation Support Experience Summary (c.1998)
author: Peter S. Kastner / Aberdeen Group
abstract: A circa-1998 summary of Aberdeen Group's litigation support experience, focusing on Peter S. Kastner's expert involvement in four legal cases: Computer Consoles vs. GTE Government Systems (RISC-Unix performance), GTE Products v. American Software (AS/400/SNA breach of contract), Unisys vs. Florida HRS (distributed IBM mainframe design), and Crossroads Software vs. Open Systems Advisors (trademark infringement). Includes case outcomes where known.
importance_rationale: Significant primary source documenting Kastner's expert witness track record across four named cases with outcomes; provides rare insight into Aberdeen's litigation consulting practice and Kastner's influence as a technical authority.
relevance_rationale: Relevant for understanding Aberdeen's consulting scope beyond market research, and Kastner's credentials as an expert witness in computer performance and systems design.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's prediction in the Unisys/HRS case that EDS's distributed system design was technically inadequate was subsequently validated when Florida cancelled the $110M contract for non-performance.
